methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Grain Weevil is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ben Johnson that has raised $3.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a broader universe of 9 private and 1 public comparable in the robotics and manufacturing sector, including peers such as 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised of $3.5M as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $9M. The peer-median method could not be reliably applied due to insufficient narrow comparables; while 12 same-sector peers exist, the intersection of same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar valuation-tier filters produced fewer than three datapoints, making median-based triangulation unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $7M (low) to $10M (high), with a median of $9M. This range reflects the application of a single methodology and carries material uncertainty given reliance on a single calibrated approach rather than triangulated cross-checks. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.5x step-up multiplier for this specific team and market segment, and the absence of direct peer-derived validation. A more robust valuation would benefit from either identifying additional seed-or-adjacent-stage robotics comparables or gathering transaction-level data from the broader peer set.
